title: Melicope eriophylla
taxonomy: Melicope eriophylla is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a species of flowering plant native to Hawaii. This plant is further classified within the genus Melicope, which comprises about 60 species of plants. The classification of Melicope eriophylla is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup. The precise classification of Melicope eriophylla is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Sapindales, Family: Rutaceae, Genus: Melicope, Species: M. eriophylla.
uses: Melicope eriophylla, commonly known as the Hawaiian mountain melicope, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's leaves and bark contain bioactive compounds that are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Melicope eriophylla is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. The plant's leaves are also used to make a tea that is said to have anti-inflammatory effects and help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

The leaves of Melicope eriophylla are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various skin conditions and infections. intro: Melicope eriophylla, also known as Hawaiian mountain garrya, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have shown potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Melicope eriophylla include flavonoids, limonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often made into a tea or infused in oil to treat various ailments, and it is also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
